raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.0,
    "R1.3": 0.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.3,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.3,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "R1.5", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Does 'make this stronger' refer to improving the writing polish (grammar, flow, conciseness), reframing the conceptual aspects (thesis, novelty), or a combination of both?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Reframing the thesis and contribution claims is a central expected responsibility to make the abstract's core message stronger and more compelling.",
    "R1.4": "Assessing and emphasizing novelty against prior work is central to strengthening the perceived impact of the presented system.",
    "R1.5": "Reorganizing the structure at the section level is peripheral but could enhance the narrative arc for a stronger overall presentation.",
    "R1.6": "Polishing sentence- and paragraph-level writing is load-bearing, as it directly improves clarity, conciseness, and persuasive flow to make the text stronger.",
    "RX.1": "Disclosing uncertainties is peripheral but nice-to-have to ensure the strengthened text remains balanced and credible.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding overclaims is load-bearing to prevent the strengthening process from introducing unsupported assertions that would invalidate the delegation.",
    "RX.3": "Adhering to the original scope is peripheral to maintain focus while making the text stronger without unnecessary expansions.",
    "RX.4": "Avoiding downstream harms is peripheral, given the technical nature of the artifact, but ensures responsible strengthening in an LLM context.",
    "RX.5": "Ensuring provenance and traceability is not directly requested but included as always active, though out of scope for this delegation."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'make this stronger' refer to improving the writing polish (grammar, flow, conciseness), reframing the conceptual aspects (thesis, novelty), or a combination of both?

rationale.R1.1: Reframing the thesis and contribution claims is a central expected responsibility to make the abstract's core message stronger and more compelling.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing and emphasizing novelty against prior work is central to strengthening the perceived impact of the presented system.
rationale.R1.5: Reorganizing the structure at the section level is peripheral but could enhance the narrative arc for a stronger overall presentation.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing sentence- and paragraph-level writing is load-bearing, as it directly improves clarity, conciseness, and persuasive flow to make the text stronger.
rationale.RX.1: Disclosing uncertainties is peripheral but nice-to-have to ensure the strengthened text remains balanced and credible.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims is load-bearing to prevent the strengthening process from introducing unsupported assertions that would invalidate the delegation.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering to the original scope is peripheral to maintain focus while making the text stronger without unnecessary expansions.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding downstream harms is peripheral, given the technical nature of the artifact, but ensures responsible strengthening in an LLM context.
rationale.RX.5: Ensuring provenance and traceability is not directly requested but included as always active, though out of scope for this delegation.
